purpose: Project-local term and replacement dictionary for cautious evidence framing in the UMC report.

HLM.first_use: hierarchical linear model (HLM), a model that separates individual-level and district-level factors in nested data
HLM.report_use: Use for multilevel association analysis and explanatory decomposition, not causal identification.

LLM.first_use: large language model (LLM), used here as a structured coding assistant for classifying platform text against predefined criteria
LLM.report_use: Use for exploratory text classification support; do not treat as an autonomous evidence producer.

Bayesian updating.first_use: Bayesian updating, a method for revising prior information with new evidence
Bayesian updating.report_use: Frame as exploratory evidence integration between administrative indicators and platform-visible signals.

multilevel association analysis.report_use: Preferred replacement for causal identification when describing HLM.

explanatory decomposition.report_use: Use when describing how HLM separates individual-level and district-level contributions.

contextual association.report_use: Preferred replacement for causal effect at the district level.

place-sensitive targeting.report_use: Policy framing that uses district indicators to locate and reach vulnerable individuals more efficiently without treating districts as causes.

place-sensitive intervention.report_use: Use when an intervention adapts people-centered support to local context.

platform-visible lived-experience signal.report_use: Use for Danggeun posts; these are visible experiences among users who chose to post, not representative prevalence estimates.

structured coding assistant.report_use: Use for the LLM role under fixed prompt criteria.

codebook.report_use: Use as an analogy for prompts that define categories and coding rules; state that it does not replace validation.

precision.definition: Among cases the LLM classified as relevant, the share that is actually relevant.
precision.report_use: Mention only as a future validation metric unless measured.

recall.definition: Among actually relevant cases, the share that the LLM identified.
recall.report_use: Mention only as a future validation metric unless measured.

F1-score.definition: A performance measure that combines precision and recall.
F1-score.report_use: Mention only as a future validation metric unless measured.

Cohen's kappa.definition: An agreement statistic for comparing two coders or a human coder and an LLM beyond chance agreement.
Cohen's kappa.report_use: Mention only as a future validation metric unless measured.

Krippendorff's alpha.definition: An agreement statistic that can handle multiple coders and different measurement levels.
Krippendorff's alpha.report_use: Mention only as a future validation metric unless measured.

Digital Desert.report_use: Use as a provisional targeting and diagnostic label, not as a fixed district identity or causal classification.

replacement_map.note: Use especially for HLM and observational results.
Avoid causal interpretation unless a design identifies it.
Districts should be framed as contexts and targeting aids.
Use except when discussing the literature's people-based/place-based distinction.

replacement_map.avoid: LLM results prove the HLM findings
use: LLM results contextualize the HLM findings
replacement_map.note: LLM analysis supplements rather than verifies the multilevel model.

replacement_map.avoid: The text analysis identifies the actual prevalence of digital deprivation
use: The text analysis identifies platform-visible signals of digital deprivation
replacement_map.note: Danggeun posts are not representative population samples.

use: LLM-assisted classification provides structured but exploratory evidence
replacement_map.note: Prompt structure improves consistency but does not replace validation.

use: Bayesian updating provides an exploratory integration of administrative indicators and platform-visible signals
replacement_map.note: Do not overstate posterior shifts as confirmation.

replacement_map.avoid: Danggeun posts reveal citizens' experiences in Seoul
use: Danggeun posts reveal platform-visible experiences among users who chose to post

A_individual_vulnerability_centered_supp: groups with low digital service-use experience
B_place_sensitive_targeting: use the UMC composite index and dimension scores to identify priority districts
do not infer district causation from low scores alone
use district indicators as aids for reaching vulnerable individuals more efficiently
